days.summary: A heavy audit and alignment day focused on E2E coverage, configurable Agent settings, Agent Team hierarchy, persona-to-clone cleanup, memory leaks, dependency pruning, and large frontend refactors. Work touched UI, Rust agent core, documentation, and test infrastructure while closing many P0/P1/P2 alignment items.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved Agent Team settings so saved controls more closely match what users see and expect during agent configuration.
Refined Agent Team hierarchy surfaces, making parent, member, and team relationships easier to inspect across configuration flows.
Cleaned up persona-to-clone naming and UI references so agent setup language feels more consistent and less confusing.
Reduced memory-leak risk in large frontend surfaces, improving stability during long-running settings, session, and Agent Team interactions.
Refactored large frontend areas and pruned unused dependencies to make settings, agent configuration, and workflow screens easier to maintain.
backendChangeBullets: Expanded E2E coverage for workspace stores, session logs, and agent configuration flows so runtime behavior is exercised more consistently.
Tightened configurable Agent settings end-to-end, reducing cases where saved values could drift from runtime behavior.
Closed several high-priority alignment items across Rust agent core, documentation, and test infrastructure.
Improved backend support for Agent Team hierarchy and configuration behavior so UI changes are backed by runtime data paths.
Removed unused dependencies and dead paths to reduce runtime surface area and simplify long-term maintenance.

days.summary: The project continued a broad configurable-surface alignment sweep across Settings, Integrations, Agent Teams, MCP, skills, databases, devtools, and wizard flows. The work emphasized removing ghost writes, adding typed contracts, surfacing errors, tightening i18n, and wiring UI controls to backend behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Aligned more Settings controls with real backend behavior so users receive clearer, more trustworthy configuration results.
Improved Integrations screens with stronger validation, clearer parsing feedback, and more visible errors when setup cannot complete.
Refined MCP setup flows so server configuration, parsing, and validation issues are easier for users to diagnose.
Improved skills setup experiences by removing unclear states and making configuration outcomes more explicit.
Tightened i18n coverage across settings, integrations, Agent Team, database, devtools, and wizard flows.
backendChangeBullets: Added stronger typed contracts around configurable surfaces so frontend payloads and backend handlers remain aligned.
Improved backend error surfacing instead of hiding failed writes or invalid configuration states.
Wired more UI controls through to backend behavior across Settings, Integrations, Agent Teams, MCP, skills, and databases.
Strengthened validation paths for MCP and skills setup so malformed inputs are caught closer to the runtime boundary.
Expanded devtools and wizard-flow coverage to verify that configuration data is submitted, persisted, and consumed correctly.

days.summary: Work centered on Agent Team and agent configuration cleanup, including SOUL. md behavior, external imports, project memory, KeyVault auth semantics, prompt-loop E2E stability, and rule/skill import paths. Several settings cleanup passes and E2E fixes reduced drift between UI state and runtime behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Clarified Agent Team memory and personality settings so users can better understand how project context and SOUL.md affect agent behavior.
Improved KeyVault authentication wording and flows for OAuth providers, including safer Cursor account setup guidance.
Cleaned up settings screens to reduce drift between visible controls and the runtime behavior they configure.
Improved prompt-loop E2E stability, making repeated agent interactions more reliable in UI-driven scenarios.
Refined external rule and skill import paths so imported assets feel more predictable from the user’s perspective.
backendChangeBullets: Improved SOUL.md handling in agent configuration so personality context is applied more consistently to the intended agent flows.
Refined external import handling for rules and skills, reducing ambiguity in how imported assets are scoped and loaded.
Improved project memory behavior so agent runtime context better reflects the workspace and selected configuration.
Tightened KeyVault OAuth semantics so token-based providers are handled distinctly from API-key providers.
Added and repaired E2E coverage around prompt loops, settings cleanup, and runtime configuration consistency.

days.summary: A major Agent Config day introduced shared runtime-limit sections, stricter capability defaults, Security tab coverage, default-off tool seeding, and many i18n/default-value cleanups. It also included broad refactors of Rust agent internals, mobile/PWA surfaces, UI settings panels, and rule import behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Added shared runtime-limit sections across Agent configuration pages so users see a more consistent control layout.
Expanded Security tab coverage, giving users clearer places to review and adjust safety-related agent settings.
Improved default-value presentation across agent settings so newly created agents start from more predictable visible states.
Refined mobile and PWA surfaces, improving layout and interaction consistency outside the primary desktop view.
Cleaned up rule import UI behavior and localized strings across agent configuration screens.
backendChangeBullets: Introduced stricter capability defaults so agents begin with safer and more predictable tool access.
Added default-off tool seeding to reduce accidental runtime access to powerful tools before users enable them.
Refactored Rust agent internals around configuration and capability handling to support the new runtime-limit model.
Improved backend support for Security tab settings so safety-related controls are backed by runtime behavior.
Expanded E2E coverage around agent configuration defaults, runtime limits, and rule import behavior.

days.summary: E2E coverage was expanded for agent imports, skills, read-only downgrades, OAuth providers, and UI reachability.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved external agent import flows with better detection for common coding assistants.
Refined skill import experiences so imported skills are easier to recognize and validate in the UI.
Improved read-only downgrade handling so users get clearer behavior when a workflow cannot run with full permissions.
Improved OAuth provider flows in setup screens, reducing confusion around account-based authentication.
Expanded UI reachability coverage so more agent and skill setup paths are exercised through rendered product flows.
backendChangeBullets: Made sub-agent model selection more reliable by resolving each member’s runtime model independently.
Expanded E2E coverage for agent imports, skills, read-only downgrade behavior, OAuth providers, and UI reachability.
Improved detection logic for external coding assistants so imported agent definitions map more accurately to runtime expectations.
Strengthened runtime handling for per-member model configuration in multi-agent workflows.
Improved validation around imported skills and agents to reduce mismatches between imported data and executable runtime state.

days.summary: Agent Teams gained runtime sessions, task-board visibility, worktree-aware subagents, and precise runtime injection of rules and skills. The codebase also saw i18n fallback removals, shared config hooks, LSP reliability fixes, and full Agent Teams page E2E coverage.
frontendChangeBullets: Exposed Agent Team member runtime sessions so users can inspect active team work more directly.
Added task-board visibility for Agent Teams, making member work and active tasks easier to follow.
Improved Agent Teams page coverage with full rendered E2E scenarios across setup and runtime views.
Removed i18n fallback behavior from visible flows so missing localized strings are easier to catch and fix.
Introduced shared configuration hooks that make Agent Team settings screens more consistent.
backendChangeBullets: Added runtime sessions for Agent Teams so individual member activity can be tracked as real executable sessions.
Improved worktree-aware subagents so member runs can operate with more accurate repository context.
Added precise runtime injection of rules and skills, ensuring agent runs receive the intended contextual tools and instructions.
Improved LSP reliability, strengthening diagnostics and code-intelligence behavior used by agent workflows.
Expanded E2E coverage for Agent Team runtime behavior, page rendering, and configuration-driven execution.

days.summary: A large UI and workflow day reshaped project/work-item surfaces, Kanban behavior, route caching, memory selection, E2E helper organization, and Agent Factory entry points. Many commits refined drag-and-drop, archive behavior, source rail layout, task scheduler flows, and rendered Kanban coverage.
frontendChangeBullets: Refined project and story workflows with clearer naming across work-item and project management screens.
Improved Kanban drag-and-drop behavior so cards move more smoothly and predictably.
Improved archive controls, making completed or inactive project items easier to manage.
Refined source rail layout to make project navigation and context selection cleaner.
Added Agent Factory entry points so users can reach agent creation and configuration flows more directly.
backendChangeBullets: Improved semantic project memory selection so agent context can draw from more relevant project information.
Adjusted route caching behavior to support faster and more stable navigation through project and work-item surfaces.
Expanded E2E helper organization around project, Kanban, and task scheduler flows for more reliable test coverage.
Improved task scheduler flow support behind the UI so scheduled work connects more cleanly to project workflows.
Strengthened rendered Kanban coverage to verify drag, archive, and detail-panel behavior through product-level tests.

days.summary: The repository continued WorkStation, Git, Kanban, and Agent Team polish. Changes improved untracked/worktree file diffs, route cache layout, tab pill states, story naming, rendered Agent Team configuration coverage, and stability around detail panels and source control interactions.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved WorkStation source control views so untracked file diffs display correctly.
Improved WorkStation handling for worktree file diffs, making branch and workspace comparisons easier to inspect.
Refreshed Agent Team creation and configuration flows with stronger rendered coverage and more stable UI states.
Improved tab pill states so active, inactive, and contextual tabs are easier to read.
Stabilized detail panels and source control interactions across WorkStation workflows.
backendChangeBullets: Improved Git diff handling for untracked files and worktree files used by WorkStation source control.
Refined route cache layout to support more predictable navigation state across WorkStation and Agent Team screens.
Expanded rendered coverage for Agent Team configuration to catch runtime and UI regressions earlier.
Strengthened source control data handling so file status and diff views remain aligned with repository state.
Improved stability around detail-panel data loading and source control interaction paths.

days.summary: This day emphasized Cursor native/provider integration, shell navigation simplification, semantic theme consolidation, branding updates, and WorkStation visual refinements. Several archived UI systems were removed while bundled app rendering and simulator configuration were stabilized.
frontendChangeBullets: Surfaced Cursor native composer support so Cursor-based sessions feel more integrated inside ORGII.
Connected Cursor subscriptions to the native session harness, making provider setup more visible to users.
Simplified shell navigation, reducing friction when moving between command and session surfaces.
Consolidated semantic theme behavior and refreshed branding for a more consistent desktop experience.
Refined WorkStation visuals and bundled app rendering so the packaged app feels more polished.
backendChangeBullets: Improved Cursor native provider integration by connecting subscription state to the native session harness.
Stabilized simulator configuration used by native and bundled app scenarios.
Removed archived UI systems and dead paths to simplify the runtime and reduce maintenance overhead.
Improved bundled app rendering support so desktop packaging paths behave more consistently.
Tightened shell navigation internals to support the simplified command and session workflow.

days.summary: Fixes dominated the day, especially around Cursor native sessions, chat rendering, session replay, source control navigation, queued follow-ups, and WorkStation control surfaces. The runtime began storing Cursor native turns as blobs and added coverage for native tool UI rendering.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved Cursor native session continuity so conversations replay more reliably after navigation or reload.
Improved native tool UI rendering so Cursor tool results appear more clearly in chat.
Made source control navigation smoother across WorkStation and session views.
Improved queued follow-up handling so pending user instructions behave more predictably in chat.
Refined WorkStation control surfaces to make session and source-control actions easier to reach.
backendChangeBullets: Started storing Cursor native turns as blobs, improving persistence for native conversation and tool-result replay.
Improved session replay for Cursor native sessions so stored turns can be reconstructed more reliably.
Added coverage for native tool UI rendering to verify backend event data appears correctly in the frontend.
Improved queued follow-up runtime handling so pending instructions survive more session states.
Strengthened source control navigation data paths used by WorkStation and session views.

days.summary: Work improved shell permission cards, slash skill/channel tools, Cursor native MCP continuation, session cache behavior, simulator follow mode, and chat panel spacing. Dead wrappers and archived ChatPanel files were removed while session replay gained a Kanban detail entry point.
frontendChangeBullets: Routed shell approvals through permission cards, giving users clearer and safer command review moments.
Improved slash skill selection so users can choose and understand agent skills more quickly from the composer.
Improved channel tool surfaces, making available communication and workflow tools easier to discover.
Refined chat panel spacing for a cleaner and more readable conversation layout.
Added a Kanban detail entry point to session replay, connecting project context more directly to replayed work.
backendChangeBullets: Improved Cursor native MCP continuation behavior so native sessions can continue tool-assisted work more reliably.
Refined session cache behavior to reduce stale or inconsistent replay state.
Improved simulator follow mode, making automated or simulated session flows easier to track.
Removed dead wrappers and archived ChatPanel files to simplify runtime and test surfaces.
Strengthened shell permission flow handling behind permission cards so command approvals carry clearer execution state.

days.summary: The day added and stabilized OAuth flows, especially Cursor OAuth in KeyVault and CLI account switching. It also optimized agent prompt cache boundaries, refreshed app-grid and editor navigation flows, routed Spotlight quick navigation through actions, and refined markdown/browser handling.
frontendChangeBullets: Added OAuth account flows in KeyVault, making account-based provider setup easier to complete from the product UI.
Improved Cursor CLI account switching isolation so users can switch accounts with fewer cross-session side effects.
Refreshed app-grid navigation, making high-level product areas easier to reach.
Improved editor navigation flows and routed Spotlight quick navigation through actions for more predictable jumps.
Refined markdown and browser handling so rendered content and opened links behave more consistently.
backendChangeBullets: Stabilized OAuth flows with special focus on Cursor OAuth and CLI account switching.
Optimized agent prompt cache boundaries so repeated conversation context loads more efficiently.
Improved account isolation for Cursor CLI runtime usage to prevent provider state from bleeding between sessions.
Routed Spotlight quick navigation through action infrastructure, aligning quick jumps with the broader command system.
Improved backend support for markdown and browser handling paths used by chat and WorkStation surfaces.

days.summary: Authentication and runtime control work continued with Codex embedded login, Claude Code OAuth runtime flows, Cursor CLI/API-key compatibility, and queued follow-up control fixes. Browser and WorkStation UI stability also improved, alongside file-size refactors for oversized hooks and components.
frontendChangeBullets: Added embedded Codex login, giving users a smoother setup path for Codex-backed sessions.
Improved Claude Code OAuth runtime support in visible authentication and session flows.
Stabilized agent stop, rewind, and forced-send controls so users can recover sessions more predictably.
Improved browser and WorkStation UI stability during authentication and runtime-control workflows.
Refactored oversized hooks and components, making large UI areas easier to maintain and less fragile.
backendChangeBullets: Added runtime support for Codex embedded login and Claude Code OAuth flows.
Improved Cursor CLI and API-key compatibility, reducing provider setup edge cases.
Fixed queued follow-up control behavior so forced sends and pending instructions align with runtime state.
Strengthened stop and rewind control handling for more predictable session recovery.
Split oversized runtime-adjacent hooks and components to reduce complexity around session control paths.

days.summary: UI consistency, Codex live behavior, Linear story/project views, settings watcher resilience, and agent control stability were the main themes. The day also added Codex live UI E2E coverage, synced workflow status controls, and refined WorkStation/sidebar/menu interactions.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved Codex live UI behavior with stronger streaming, rewind, and OAuth handling.
Added native Linear project views so users can inspect Linear-linked project data directly inside ORGII.
Synchronized workflow status controls, making status changes more consistent across project and workflow surfaces.
Refined WorkStation, sidebar, and menu interactions for smoother navigation.
Added Codex live UI E2E coverage to protect streaming and authentication experiences.
backendChangeBullets: Improved Codex live runtime behavior for streaming, rewind, and OAuth-backed execution.
Strengthened settings watcher resilience so configuration updates are handled more reliably.
Improved backend support for native Linear project data used by the new project views.
Synchronized workflow status data paths so UI controls and runtime state stay aligned.
Expanded E2E coverage for Codex live behavior and agent control stability.

days.summary: Plan mode and approval flows were stabilized, Codex streaming improved, and WorkStation chrome received repeated refinement. The day also unified DB table preview surfaces, added Linear project overview editing, split settings route cache roots, and modularized DevTools previews.
frontendChangeBullets: Stabilized plan mode approval flows so users can review and approve agent plans with fewer confusing states.
Improved Codex streaming feedback for smoother live collaboration.
Refined WorkStation chrome through repeated layout and interaction polish.
Added Linear project overview editing, allowing project details to be updated from the product surface.
Unified database table preview surfaces so database records are displayed more consistently.
backendChangeBullets: Improved plan mode approval runtime handling so approval state is more reliable across agent sessions.
Strengthened Codex streaming behavior in the runtime path behind live chat updates.
Unified DB table preview data handling to support consistent database table rendering.
Split settings route cache roots to make settings navigation state more stable.
Modularized DevTools previews, reducing coupling between diagnostic surfaces and runtime data sources.

days.summary: A smaller day focused on E2E spec organization, streaming history stability, OAuth refresh ordering, plan rewind behavior, and WorkStation/Ops Control layout refinements. The Tauri updater dependency was pinned and session cache refresh changes were rolled back.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved streaming session history so previous live responses replay with fewer ordering issues.
Refined WorkStation layout for cleaner navigation and more stable control placement.
Refined Ops Control layout so operational information is easier to scan.
Improved plan rewind behavior, making plan-based sessions easier to recover and inspect.
Reorganized E2E specs, making UI coverage easier to maintain and reason about.
backendChangeBullets: Tightened OAuth refresh ordering to reduce authentication timing issues during provider-backed sessions.
Improved plan lifecycle ordering so replay, rewind, and continued execution remain consistent.
Pinned the Tauri updater dependency to keep desktop update behavior stable.
Rolled back session cache refresh changes that caused undesirable runtime behavior.
Improved streaming history persistence and replay paths used by live session views.

days.summary: Work refined Launchpad/App Launcher integration, plan approval semantics, Gemini OAuth, Cursor mode switching, branch history loading, and WorkStation sidebar localization. Several fixes restored isolated launch states and exports while tightening pending-plan skip behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Renamed App Launcher to Launchpad, making the product entry point clearer and more intentional.
Improved isolated launch behavior so new launches start from a cleaner and more predictable state.
Added Cursor mode switching, giving users more direct control over Cursor-backed session behavior.
Improved WorkStation sidebar localization so navigation labels are clearer across languages.
Refined branch history loading in the UI so repository history appears more reliably.
backendChangeBullets: Added Gemini OAuth support for Google-backed agent flows.
Tightened plan approval semantics, including clearer handling for pending-plan skip behavior.
Improved isolated launch state handling so runtime launches do not inherit unintended context.
Restored exports needed by launch, session, or integration paths.
Improved branch history loading support behind repository and WorkStation views.

days.summary: Gemini native and CLI integration reached a more complete state while inline canvas tooling was introduced and threaded into agents, simulator messages, notification cards, and i18n. Additional work optimized grep, tightened core audit issues, and refined Story Manager surfaces.
frontendChangeBullets: Completed Gemini native and CLI integration for a more reliable Google agent experience in ORGII.
Introduced inline canvas rendering for agents, simulator messages, and notification cards.
Improved notification cards so canvas-backed content can be presented more richly.
Refined Story Manager surfaces, improving the way project stories and related context are displayed.
Expanded localized UI text around canvas and Gemini-related flows.
backendChangeBullets: Completed Gemini native and CLI runtime integration, strengthening both direct and command-line Google agent paths.
Threaded inline canvas tooling into agent runtime outputs, simulator messages, and notification-card data.
Optimized grep behavior used by agent and tooling workflows, improving search reliability and responsiveness.
Tightened core audit issues across runtime and tooling code paths.
Improved the inline canvas tool matrix so supported canvas actions are represented more consistently.

days.summary: The project added org hub and diary/Gantt timeline surfaces, improved context-panel metrics and UI, parsed Cursor subagent prompts as events, and refined Linear/project detail caching. Large frontend files were split and the inline canvas/tool matrix received more hardening.
frontendChangeBullets: Added diary-style timelines for sessions and commits, giving users a chronological way to inspect work.
Added Gantt-style timeline surfaces for a more structured view of sessions, commits, and project activity.
Improved the context panel with richer breakdowns and more useful metrics.
Improved chat input placement for better accessibility and easier conversation flow.
Refined Linear and project detail caching behavior so project pages feel more responsive.
backendChangeBullets: Parsed Cursor subagent prompts as events, improving structured runtime visibility into subagent activity.
Improved context-panel metric data so richer breakdowns can be displayed in the UI.
Hardened the inline canvas and tool matrix paths used by agent outputs.
Improved Linear and project detail caching support for faster repeated access to project data.
Split large frontend files tied to runtime-heavy surfaces, reducing complexity around timeline and context features.

days.summary: E2E runtime coverage was consolidated and pruned while OAuth recovery, plan recovery, control matrix selection, and tool metadata schemas were stabilized. UI work added live streaming HUDs, tool-result copy/jump actions, inline policy/tool detail cards, and polished integrations and agent settings tables.
frontendChangeBullets: Added live chat streaming metrics, including elapsed time, token rate, and estimated completion.
Improved tool result cards with copy actions so users can quickly reuse generated outputs.
Added source jumps from tool results, making it easier to move from an agent result to the relevant file or location.
Added inline policy and tool detail cards for clearer explanations of agent actions.
Polished integrations and agent settings tables for better scanning, alignment, and control feedback.
backendChangeBullets: Consolidated and pruned E2E runtime coverage to focus tests on the most valuable execution paths.
Improved OAuth recovery behavior so provider-backed sessions can recover more reliably after authentication interruptions.
Improved plan recovery handling, reducing failures when plan-driven sessions need to continue or resume.
Stabilized control matrix selection so runtime controls map more reliably to the available session actions.
Stabilized tool metadata schemas so tool-result cards, copy actions, and source jumps receive better-structured data.

days.summary: Focused E2E stabilization continued for account switching, Gemini CLI fallback detection, OAuth refresh persistence, and run_shell cwd behavior. The UI consolidated Rules, Memory, and Evolution settings, moved External IDE controls, rendered agent_send_message tool cards, and improved plan/chat progress defaults.
frontendChangeBullets: Consolidated Rules, Memory, and Evolution into a clearer shared settings surface.
Moved External IDE controls to a more appropriate settings location, reducing configuration clutter.
Rendered agent_send_message tool cards so agent-to-agent messages are easier to understand in chat.
Improved plan and chat progress defaults so users see clearer progress states during plan workflows.
Improved account switching and Gemini CLI fallback feedback through focused E2E stabilization.
backendChangeBullets: Stabilized account switching behavior across provider-backed sessions.
Improved Gemini CLI fallback detection so runtime can better identify when command-line Gemini support is unavailable.
Improved OAuth refresh persistence so refreshed authentication state is retained more reliably.
Fixed run_shell cwd behavior so shell commands execute in the intended working directory.
Improved runtime metadata for agent_send_message so tool cards can render agent communication accurately.

days.summary: Session persistence and E2E controls were hardened, including non-streaming assistant tool text, failed turn events, rewind ownership, and filtered scenario handling. The day also added chat model-picker toggles, inline preview cards, image/multimodal diagnostics, and simplified built-in agent autonomy defaults.
frontendChangeBullets: Added model-picker toggles, making chat configuration easier to access from the conversation flow.
Added inline preview cards so users can inspect rich content without leaving the current session.
Improved chat handling for image and multimodal diagnostics, making media-related issues easier to understand.
Simplified built-in agent autonomy defaults so agent setup feels clearer and less overloaded.
Improved rewind ownership behavior in the UI so recovery actions are easier to reason about.
backendChangeBullets: Improved session persistence so non-streaming assistant tool text is retained correctly.
Persisted failed turn events more reliably, making session history and diagnostics more complete.
Hardened E2E controls around session persistence, rewind ownership, and filtered scenarios.
Improved multimodal diagnostic handling for image-related session workflows.
Simplified built-in agent autonomy defaults in runtime configuration to reduce unexpected behavior.

days.summary: WorkStation gained All Tabs dock navigation, cross-host tab behavior, tighter header strips, and a QQ-style presence pill that feeds agent context. Agent and CLI plan E2E coverage was hardened, process-group termination was fixed, and legacy WorkStation literals/atoms were deleted.
frontendChangeBullets: Added All Tabs dock navigation in WorkStation for easier cross-host tab switching.
Improved cross-host tab behavior so users can move between WorkStation contexts more reliably.
Tightened WorkStation header strips for a cleaner and denser workspace layout.
Introduced a presence pill that shows availability context and feeds it into agent sessions.
Removed legacy WorkStation literals and atoms to simplify the visible workspace experience.
backendChangeBullets: Fed presence pill availability context into agent sessions so agents can account for user availability.
Hardened Agent and CLI plan E2E coverage for more reliable plan-driven execution.
Fixed process-group termination so spawned processes are stopped more safely and completely.
Improved cross-host tab state handling behind WorkStation navigation.
Deleted legacy WorkStation state paths to reduce stale runtime behavior and simplify data flow.

days.summary: Agent Team intervention and inbox UI coverage expanded while settings tables, chat-panel layout, modal/dropdown layering, and context-menu behavior were polished. The day also locked Agent Team evidence classification rules and improved owner session jump and hierarchy UI coverage.
frontendChangeBullets: Added Agent Team intervention state so users can more clearly see when human input or oversight is involved.
Improved Agent Team inbox coverage in both directions, making member and owner communication more reliable.
Polished Agent Team message layout for clearer conversations and better readability.
Improved settings tables, modal layering, dropdown behavior, and context-menu interactions.
Improved owner session jump and hierarchy UI coverage so users can navigate Agent Team structure more confidently.
backendChangeBullets: Locked Agent Team evidence classification rules so runtime evidence is categorized more consistently.
Improved Agent Team inbox behavior in both owner-to-member and member-to-owner directions.
Strengthened intervention state handling so Agent Team runtime can represent oversight and interruption states.
Expanded E2E coverage for owner session jumps and Agent Team hierarchy behavior.
Improved context-menu and modal interaction coverage to protect complex layered UI states.

days.summary: Agent Team runtime became the dominant focus, with direct member messaging, launch snapshots, wake semantics, task queue evidence, atomic claims, member state evidence, and rich card rendering. Routine launches were unified and E2E coverage advanced across gist workflows, task history, and subagent boundary behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Added direct Agent Team member messaging so users can communicate with individual team members more naturally.
Added richer Agent Team cards for clearer team coordination and task visibility.
Improved Agent Team task history views, making prior work easier to inspect.
Advanced gist workflow coverage so shared summaries and captured task context behave more reliably.
Unified routine launch experiences so starting repeated Agent Team work feels more consistent.
backendChangeBullets: Added Agent Team launch snapshots so runtime starts can capture the relevant team and task state.
Improved wake semantics so Agent Team members can be activated more predictably when new work arrives.
Added task queue evidence and member state evidence for richer runtime tracking of Agent Team activity.
Added atomic task claims to reduce conflicts when multiple members coordinate around queued work.
Strengthened subagent boundary behavior so member tasks remain better isolated during execution.

days.summary: Agent Team member identity and inbox isolation were heavily repaired, covering stale task release, sender preservation, member-aware debug harnesses, intervention scoping, and task result rendering. Additional UI work adjusted RAM details, playground effects, and run-view polling.
frontendChangeBullets: Strengthened Agent Team member inbox isolation so messages stay scoped to the correct agent.
Improved task result rendering, making completed member work easier to understand in the UI.
Adjusted RAM details displays so resource information is clearer and better aligned.
Refined playground effects for smoother interactive experimentation surfaces.
Improved run-view polling behavior so active Agent Team runs update more reliably.
backendChangeBullets: Repaired Agent Team member identity handling so messages and task results preserve the correct sender.
Improved stale-task release so abandoned or outdated tasks can be freed more safely.
Added member-aware debug harnesses to test Agent Team behavior with clearer member identity context.
Improved intervention scoping so oversight events apply to the intended member or task.
Improved run-view polling data paths for more accurate active-run updates.

days.summary: Agent Team audit cleanup continued with CLI member launch fixes, task event schema repair, team-mode semantics, intervention regressions, rendered E2E stabilization, and context row controls. The WorkStation and chat UI also received navigation, composer, bottom-panel, and rendering pipeline refinements.
frontendChangeBullets: Refined WorkStation navigation for smoother movement between workspace areas and active sessions.
Improved chat composer behavior so message entry and action selection feel more stable.
Refined bottom-panel rendering, improving layout stability for secondary session information.
Added context row controls that make session and project context easier to manage.
Stabilized rendered E2E coverage around Agent Team and chat interactions.
backendChangeBullets: Fixed Agent Team CLI member launches so command-line members start with the intended runtime configuration.
Repaired task event schemas so Agent Team task progress and evidence can be handled consistently.
Improved team-mode semantics, clarifying how member runs participate in shared Agent Team work.
Fixed task progress initialization so new work starts with accurate runtime progress state.
Improved intervention regressions and subagent runtime handling through focused audit cleanup.

days.summary: Agent Team pause/resume, restart persistence, sidebar retention, member launch overrides, history resume, and idle wakeups were repaired and tested. E2E coverage shifted toward UI-driven pause/resume clicks, app restart persistence, and sidebar session persistence rather than direct API shortcuts.
frontendChangeBullets: Replaced Agent Team stopping with clearer pause and resume controls.
Persisted Agent Team run state across app restarts so users can return to active team work.
Improved sidebar session restoration, keeping Agent Team sessions visible after restart.
Shifted E2E coverage toward real UI pause and resume clicks, matching how users actually control runs.
Improved history resume behavior so users can continue prior Agent Team work more naturally.
backendChangeBullets: Repaired Agent Team pause and resume runtime behavior so runs can be controlled without losing state.
Improved restart persistence for Agent Team runs, retaining enough runtime state to restore active work.
Improved member launch overrides so Agent Team members start with the correct launch-specific settings.
Repaired idle wakeups so inactive members can resume work when new tasks or messages arrive.
Expanded E2E coverage for app restart persistence and sidebar session persistence through product-level flows.

days.summary: Agent Team group chat routing became the main focus, including default group view, mentions, non-interrupting routing, and collapse scoping.
frontendChangeBullets: Defaulted Agent Team chats to group view so team conversations are visible first.
Added non-interrupting group chat routing, letting users message the team without disrupting active work unnecessarily.
Improved mention behavior in Agent Team group chats so messages can target the right participants.
Added an always-on native island bar for persistent high-level session and workspace awareness.
Improved repo, branch, and Worktree selection menus for clearer workspace targeting.
backendChangeBullets: Improved Agent Team group chat routing so default group conversations are handled as first-class runtime events.
Added non-interrupting routing behavior so group chat messages can be delivered without forcing active tasks to stop.
Improved mention parsing and routing so targeted Agent Team messages reach the intended participants.
Improved collapse scoping so grouped Agent Team conversation state remains organized correctly.
Strengthened repository, branch, and Worktree selection data paths used by launch and workspace workflows.

days.summary: Agent Team group chat live behavior was restored and improved, including bubble streams, progress bubbles, activity visibility, transcript semantics, wakeups, mentions, and pause flow. The day also added Benchmark Runner modes/task browsing, Session JSON import/export, and WorkStation placeholder/status polish.
frontendChangeBullets: Restored live Agent Team group chat bubbles so team conversations update in real time again.
Added clearer progress bubbles and activity visibility for Agent Team group chat.
Improved Agent Team pause flow so users can control live group work with less confusion.
Added Benchmark Runner execution modes and task browsing for more flexible benchmarking workflows.
Added Session JSON import and export, plus WorkStation placeholder and status polish.
backendChangeBullets: Restored Agent Team group chat live behavior, including bubble streams and progress event handling.
Improved transcript semantics so group chat records better reflect user turns, member activity, and progress.
Improved wakeups and mentions in group chat runtime so members can react to new messages correctly.
Added Benchmark Runner modes and task browsing support behind the new benchmark UI.
Added Session JSON import and export support for moving or inspecting session data.

days.summary: The month closed with Agent Team round-based group chat restoration, user-turn and round-routing fixes, external rule import refinements, settings shortcuts, chat reference cards, directory explorer tabs, profile context injection, macOS Git bundling, and final WorkStation/status layout polish.
frontendChangeBullets: Fixed Agent Team group chat user turns and round-based routing so team conversations feel more coherent.
Added settings keyboard shortcuts for faster navigation and configuration access.
Added chat reference cards so important context can be shown directly inside conversations.
Added directory explorer tabs, making filesystem browsing more flexible inside ORGII.
Polished WorkStation and status layouts for a cleaner end-of-month user experience.
backendChangeBullets: Restored Agent Team round-based group chat behavior so member turns can be coordinated by conversation rounds.
Refined external rule import behavior so agent instructions from outside ORGII are loaded more accurately.
Added user profile context injection so agents can receive relevant user context during runtime.
Bundled Git for macOS, improving baseline Git availability for desktop workflows.
Fixed round routing and user-turn handling in Agent Team group chat runtime.
